confirmed_ok: REST namespace sniper/v1 correct
EA route POST /ea/market-stream registered with permission_ea_market_stream
permission_ea_market_stream: reads all 4 header aliases (x-ea-api-key, x_ea_api_key, x-api-key, x_api_key)
permission_ea_market_stream: hash_equals() used — no == or === comparison
permission_ea_market_stream: missing token → 401
permission_ea_market_stream: unconfigured secret → 503 + error_log
permission_ea_market_stream: invalid token → 403
permission_ea_market_stream: missing user_id → 400
permission_ea_market_stream: invalid user_id → 403
permission_ea_market_stream: wp_set_current_user() called before returning true
authority-diagnostics uses permission_user → 401 unauthenticated (correct, not a bug)
admin/* routes use permission_admin → requires manage_options
bid/ask validation: is_finite() + > 0 + bid <= ask
validate_ohlc(): high >= max(open, close) && low <= min(open, close)
guard_tick_volume(): negative values clamped to 0 with audit
Candle epoch guard: timestamps pre-2000 rejected
M1 candle staleness: 180s max_age_sec
M15 candle staleness: 1800s max_age_sec
Snapshot broker timestamp preserved (not server time)
Signal engine gates on: price_state=live, candle count >= 30, candle age <= 7200s, chop < 0.7
FreshnessBadge.tsx: state from backend price.state — not computed from page-load time
VerdictBadge.tsx: verdict from backend signal — no frontend override
useSniperData.ts: polling gated on backendReady && pollMs !== null
sniperClient.ts call(): throws on 401, never swallows errors
EA payload contract consistent: EA sends timestamp, PHP reads timestamp
EA payload contract consistent: EA sends candle{} + candle_m15{}, PHP reads them as objects
MT5 EA TimeToIso8601: UTC conversion via broker offset — consistent with PHP strtotime()

confirmed_issues.description: When timestamp is absent from the EA payload but candles are present, $stream_timestamp becomes null. The open/future candle guard (if ($stream_timestamp && strtotime(candle.time) >= strtotime($stream_timestamp))) is skipped, allowing a forming/open candle (not yet closed) to be stored. The staleness age check (age_seconds > max_age_sec) is also bypassed entirely.
confirmed_issues.root_cause: insert_mt5_candle() receives $stream_timestamp = $payload['timestamp'] ?? null. Both the future-candle guard and the age staleness guard are wrapped in if ($stream_timestamp) blocks. When null, both checks are skipped.
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: When $stream_timestamp is null and a candle is present, fall back to time() (server clock) as $stream_timestamp. This ensures the future-candle and staleness guards are always applied. The EA always sends timestamp, so this only affects non-standard callers.

confirmed_issues.system: EA Market Stream Handler — HTTP response semantics
confirmed_issues.description: Stale payload rejection returns HTTP 400 (Bad Request) instead of HTTP 422 (Unprocessable Entity). Per the workflow spec and REST semantics, a stale quote_time is a business logic / data validity failure, not a malformed request. The error code 'stale_data' is correct but the HTTP status is wrong.
confirmed_issues.root_cause: post_ea_market_stream() calls new WP_Error('stale_data', ..., array('status' => 400)) for both the unparseable-timestamp and age-exceeded rejection paths.
impact_zones: EA retry logic (semantic clarity only — EA does not branch on 400 vs 422)
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Change status from 400 to 422 for both stale_data rejections. Update regression test to assert code 422.

confirmed_issues.description: Test 3 (missing user_id) in test-ea-market-stream.php is labeled 'Test 2' due to a copy-paste mistake at line 433. This is a cosmetic test documentation error.
confirmed_issues.root_cause: Copy-paste typo in test label string.
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Fix label to 'Test 3: Missing user_id rejected by EA permission gate'.

parity_status.known_spec_divergence: Spec uses quote_time/candles[] but actual contract uses timestamp/candle{}. Both EA and PHP are aligned to the actual contract — spec is aspirational.
